metadata.author: Shailesh Wankar
metadata.design_notes: IR plan alone scores 15 = Low, mirroring SOC2 (also 15). Scenarios escalate from isolated governance gap to active threat with zero response capability. No Critical in this category — four Criticals already exist across IAM, DP, VM, CG.

scenarios.input: A vendor providing time and attendance tracking software for workforce management has no documented incident response plan and no breach notification procedure. No other security control gaps were identified. All other controls including MFA, encryption, annual penetration testing, and compliance documentation are in place and evidenced.
scenarios.notes: no_ir_plan=15. Total=15. Rating: Low. Mirrors CG-001 pattern — isolated governance gap is Low. Tests that IR gap alone does not inflate to Medium. Vendor narrative confirms all other controls are compliant.

scenarios.input: A cloud fax and secure messaging vendor transmitting Protected Health Information on behalf of healthcare providers has no documented incident response plan and no breach notification procedure compliant with regulatory requirements. The vendor cannot provide SOC2 Type II or ISO 27001 certification. PHI is transmitted through the vendor platform daily.
scenarios.notes: no_ir_plan=15, no_soc2_iso_evidence=15. Total=30. Rating: Medium. Two governance gaps — no response plan and no compliance certification. Tests both Incident Response Policy and Compliance Mapping Guide citation.

scenarios.input: A vendor providing application performance monitoring with read access to production logs, error traces, and API response data has no documented incident response plan. If the monitoring platform is compromised, no defined containment or notification procedure exists. The vendor has not conducted an annual penetration test in 17 months and has no test currently scheduled.
scenarios.notes: no_ir_plan=15, no_annual_pentest=20. Total=35. Rating: Medium. No response plan combined with untested attack surface. If the monitoring platform is compromised, the vendor will not detect or respond to it.

scenarios.input: A vendor managing customer loyalty programme data including purchase history, points balances, and stored payment card references for a retail chain has no incident response plan. MFA is not enforced on any user or admin account accessing the loyalty platform. Customer data including payment card references is stored unencrypted at rest.
scenarios.notes: no_ir_plan=15, mfa_missing=25, no_encryption=30. Total=70. Rating: High. Three-control FM-005 scenario. A breach is easy to execute (no MFA, unencrypted data) and impossible to respond to (no IR plan). Three distinct source documents expected.

scenarios.sub_type: IR + CVE + Data — High (active threat, zero response)
scenarios.input: A vendor providing industrial control system monitoring for manufacturing facilities stores operational telemetry data from factory floor equipment. The vendor has no incident response plan — a compromise of the monitoring platform would have no defined containment, notification, or recovery procedure. Critical CVE vulnerabilities on the monitoring servers have not been patched despite vendor notification 60 days ago. All operational data stored by the platform is unencrypted at rest.
scenarios.notes: no_ir_plan=15, critical_cve_exposure=30, no_encryption=30. Total=75. Rating: High. Active exploitable vulnerability, unprotected data, and zero response capability — three conditions defining a worst-case breach scenario. FM-005: all three controls required. Three source documents.
